title: Top This: National Hat Day!
story: People across the country put on their wildest, fanciest, or most beloved hats every January 15 for National Hat Day! From baseball caps and beanies to fancy fedoras and silly sombreros, everyone gets to show off their favorite headwear. Schools often have hat parades where kids wear crazy creations made from cardboard, feathers, and glitter. Social media fills up with selfies of people sporting everything from vintage top hats to modern snapbacks. Some folks even dig out old family hats or try on styles they've never worn before, making this quirky holiday a perfect excuse to top off any outfit with personality!
activity_question: What do schools often organize on National Hat Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most unusual hat you've ever seen someone wear?

title: Wikipedia Day: The World's Biggest Brain Share
story: Every January 15, people around the world celebrate Wikipedia Day, honoring the free online encyclopedia that anyone can edit! On this day, Wikipedia fans share their favorite weird articles, donate money to keep the site running, and thank the millions of volunteers who write and edit pages. Teachers use it to show students how knowledge gets shared, while internet users post screenshots of the strangest Wikipedia rabbit holes they've fallen down. It's amazing that this giant collection of human knowledge started as just an idea and now helps billions of people learn something new every single day.
activity_question: What makes Wikipedia special compared to other encyclopedias?
activity_choices: Anyone can edit and add to it
It only has articles about animals
You have to pay money to read it
activity_answer: Anyone can edit and add to it
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest or most interesting Wikipedia article you've ever stumbled across?
